values.content:
=You are a compliance analyst.

Analyze the following regulatory update and generate a structured compliance response.

Input:
Title: {{ $json.title }}
Published Date: {{ $json.pubDate }}
Category: {{ $json.category }}
Source Link: {{ $json.link }}
Content: {{ $json.briefText }}

Instructions:
1. Determine whether this update is actionable for a company compliance team.
2. Focus on concrete tax, reporting, filing, invoicing, enforcement, penalty, disclosure or policy impacts.
3. If the update mainly reports a legal or regulatory change with potential business impact, treat it as actionable.
4. If the update is informational only and does not imply a likely compliance action, treat it as not actionable.

If NOT actionable, return:
{
  "actionable": "No",
  "reason": "short reason",
  "summary": "brief summary",
  "priority": "Low",
  "checklist": [],
  "owner_team": "Monitor",
  "due_timeline": "monitor"
}

If actionable, return:
{
  "actionable": "Yes",
  "reason": "why this requires action",
  "summary": "2-3 sentence summary of the update",
  "priority": "High or Medium or Low",
  "checklist": [
    "specific action 1",
    "specific action 2",
    "specific action 3"
  ],
  "owner_team": "Compliance or Finance or Legal or IT",
  "due_timeline": "immediate or 7 days or 30 days or monitor"
}

Rules:
- Return ONLY valid JSON
- Do NOT include markdown or explanations
- Checklist must contain specific actionable steps based on the provided content
- Do not use generic checklist items unless clearly supported by the content
- Base your reasoning primarily on the Content field, using the title and category as supporting context

parameters.content:
#  Compliance Checklist Auto-Generator

##  How it works:
This workflow monitors a tax-focused RSS feed and automatically converts regulatory updates into structured compliance checklists. When a new item is detected, it filters for relevant tax and compliance content, processes each article individually and fetches its full page to extract a concise “In brief” summary. An AI model then analyzes this content to determine whether the update is actionable for a compliance team. If actionable, it generates a summary, reason, priority and a checklist of required actions along with the responsible team and timeline. The final output is stored in Google Sheets for tracking and follow-up.


##  Setup steps:
1. Create a Google Sheet with columns such as Date Added, Published Date, Title, Category, Link, GUID, Summary, Reason, Priority, Checklist, Owner Team, Due Timeline and Status.
2. Connect your Google Sheets account in n8n and select the target spreadsheet and sheet.
3. Configure your Gemini (Google AI) API credentials to enable AI-based analysis.
4. Configure the RSS feed URL for a trusted tax or regulatory updates source (e.g., Baker McKenzie tax insights), ensuring it provides relevant compliance-focused content.
5. Verify the HTML extraction selector matches the “In brief” section of your source articles.
6. Adjust the RSS polling interval (e.g., hourly) and wait time between items (e.g., 30–120 seconds) to manage API usage.
7. Test the workflow with a few items to ensure correct parsing and sheet updates.
8. Activate the workflow to start automatically monitoring and logging compliance updates.

## RSS Monitoring & Relevance Filtering
Automatically monitors tax compliance RSS feed for new updates. Filters incoming items using keyword logic to retain only relevant regulatory and compliance-related articles for further processing.

## Article Processing & Extraction
Processes articles one by one, fetches full content and extracts the “In brief” section. Structures key fields like title, date, category and content for AI analysis.

## AI Compliance Analysis
Sends structured article data to Gemini AI to evaluate whether the update is actionable. Parses the JSON response and extracts summary, priority, checklist, owner team and timeline.

## Action Filtering & Logging
Filters actionable updates, formats them into structured rows and logs them into Google Sheets. Skips non-actionable items and controls execution flow using a wait node.
